title: draba × salomonii
intro: Draba × salomonii, also known as Salomon's draba or Salomon's whitlowgrass, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a hybrid of Draba alpina and Draba nemorosa, characterized by its small, white, four-petaled flowers and narrow, grass-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of Draba × salomonii can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams to harness its health benefits, which include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function.
